sections.paragraph:
ὑπεναντίος, α, ον (Hes., Hdt. et al.) mostly opposed, contrary, hostile (Thu. 2, 2, 2; Pla., Tht. 176a; Demosth. 24, 108 al.; ins [e.g. ISmyrnaMcCabe 0421, 8], pap; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 180) τινί against someone Col 2:14. Subst. ὁ ὑπεναντίος the opponent (X.; Polyb. 1, 11, 14; Plut., Thes. 6 [13, 2]; ins; POxy 1151, 55; LXX; TestSol 8:10; predom. in pl.) οἱ ὑπεναντίοι the adversaries (X., Cyr. 1, 6, 38) of God Hb 10:27 (cp. Is 26:11).—DELG s.v. ἄντα. M-M.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

ISmyrnaMcCabe ISmyrnaMcCabe = Smyrna Inscriptions, Texts and List, prepared by DMcCabe et al.—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

  1. pl. = plural
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1030
